---
pagetitle: "Arnaud Raulet | Data Scientist · France-Japan"
description: "Data scientist based in Tokyo, I analyse the data hidden behind language barriers or methodological gaps, and turn them into quantified recommendations."
lang: en
format:
  html:
    page-layout: article
    toc: false
body-classes: home
---

:::: column-screen
::: hero-banner
::: hero-title
The data nobody else analyses, for the decisions that matter.
:::

::: hero-subtitle
Data scientist based in Tokyo, I analyse the data hidden behind language barriers or methodological gaps — Japanese patents, pricing signals, marketing performance — and turn them into quantified recommendations.
:::

[Book a call](/contact/){.btn .btn-primary .btn-lg} [See my services](/services/index.en.html){.btn .btn-outline-secondary .btn-lg}
:::
::::

## The problem

You have data. But do you have answers?

::: {.column-page}
:::::: grid
::: g-col-md-6
**Your Japanese competitors are filing patents, signing alliances, investing in R&D. And you don't see it.**

The data is public, freely available on J-PlatPat. But it's in Japanese, and interpreting it requires a rare combination of technical and linguistic expertise.

*How many strategic decisions are you making without seeing what your Japanese competitors are actually doing?*

[JapanTechRadar →](/services/japan-tech-radar.en.html)
:::

::: g-col-md-6
**Is your pricing really optimal?**

Most pricing policies are built on intuition and history, never properly reviewed with structured data. Underpriced products can subsidise your customers' margins for years without any clear alarm signal.

*Knowing exactly where your prices underperform, by how much, and what to do about it changes the decision.*

[PricingDiag →](/services/pricing-diag.en.html)
:::
::::::

:::::: grid
::: g-col-md-6
**Your campaign generated +15% in sales. Did it, really?**

Or was the market already rising? Without rigorous method, you're optimising in the dark. Dashboards show numbers — they don't show causes.

*Misattributed performance: you've been allocating budget to the wrong channels for months.*

[Marketing Analytics →](/services/index.en.html#marketing-analytics)
:::

::: g-col-md-6
**You see a correlation in your data. But is that the actual cause?**

Acting on the wrong lever means investing in the wrong place. Causal inference separates what truly causes a result from what is merely correlated with it.

*A decision built on a spurious correlation can cost months of wasted optimisation.*

[Decision Science →](/services/index.en.html#decision-science)
:::
::::::
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## What I bring

::: {.column-page}
:::::: grid
::: g-col-md-6
### Japan Technology Intelligence

What your Japanese competitors are doing: extracted from patent data, quantified, delivered in English. Actor mapping, technology trends, weak signals.

[Discover JapanTechRadar →](/services/japan-tech-radar.en.html)
:::

::: g-col-md-6
### Pricing Diagnostic

Where your prices underperform, by how much, and what to do about it. In 2 weeks, using your existing sales data. Prioritised recommendations, simulation file included.

[Discover PricingDiag →](/services/pricing-diag.en.html)
:::
::::::

:::::: grid
::: g-col-md-6
### Marketing Analytics

Which channel is really driving your conversions? What is the true ROI of each campaign? Quantified answers grounded in your data, not approximations.

[See services →](/services/index.en.html#marketing-analytics)
:::

::: g-col-md-6
### Causal Inference & Decision Science

Identifying what truly causes a result, not just what correlates with it. So you invest in the right lever, with an explicit quantification of uncertainty.

[See services →](/services/index.en.html#decision-science)
:::
::::::
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Who is this for?

I work with:

- **Leaders of SMEs and mid-size companies** who want quantified answers, not more reports
- **Marketing directors** who want to measure what actually works in their mix
- **Commercial directors** who suspect their pricing policy is not optimal
- **European companies** who need to understand what Japanese players in their sector are doing

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## My approach

**Primary data. Rigorous methods. Actionable recommendations.**

I work on primary data, not second-hand reports. Each analysis combines structured data extraction (patents, transactions, marketing data), rigorous quantitative methods, and contextual interpretation.

The result: quantified recommendations with an explicit measure of uncertainty. You will know what you can assert, and what remains to be verified.

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| fig-width: 10
#| fig-height: 6
#| fig-align: center

library(ggplot2)
set.seed(69)
n <- 60
rain <- rbeta(n, 2, 5) * 100
umbrellas <- 200 + 8 * rain + rnorm(n, sd = 50)
coffees <- 1000 + 15 * rain + rnorm(n, sd = 100)
data <- data.frame(umbrellas = umbrellas, coffees = coffees, rain = rain)

ggplot(data, aes(x = umbrellas, y = coffees)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = rain), size = 4, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#e63946", linetype = "dashed", alpha = 0.2) +
  scale_color_gradient(low = "#f4a261", high = "#457b9d", name = "Rainy days (%)") +
  labs(
    title = "Correlation ≠ Causation",
    subtitle = "Umbrella sales correlate with coffee consumption.\nShould you distribute umbrellas to sell more coffee?",
    x = "Umbrella sales",
    y = "Coffees consumed",
    caption = "The real cause: the weather. My work: identifying these hidden variables in your data."
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 20, hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "gray40", size = 13, hjust = 0.5, lineheight = 1.2),
    plot.caption = element_text(color = "gray50", face = "italic", size = 11),
    legend.position = "right",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Background

::::: grid
::: g-col-md-6
**Nearly 10 years** in Japan, across demanding sectors: defence, aerospace, automotive, industry

**Trilingual** French, Japanese, English: technical patent reading, nuanced understanding of Japanese business

**Former patent translator** JP to FR for the automotive industry
:::

::: g-col-md-6
**Certified** UC Berkeley (Marketing Analytics), Harvard (Data Science)

**Based in Tokyo**, working with clients in France, Japan and internationally

**Data Scientist** specialised in causal inference and Bayesian modelling
:::
:::::

[About me →](/about.en.html)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Latest articles

[See all articles →](/blog/index.en.html)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

::: contact-section
## Let's talk about your situation

Book a 30-minute discovery call. We identify together whether I can help you, and how.

No pitch. No commitment. Just a conversation.

[Book a discovery call](/contact/){.btn .btn-primary .btn-lg} [Email me](mailto:contact-quarto-site.le77r@simplelogin.com){.btn .btn-outline-secondary .btn-lg}
:::
